codeReview.summary: The feature wires persisted theme/font preferences through settings, app chrome, and live session font metrics, but it does not actually theme the terminal surface itself, so an already-open or restored session still shows a hard-coded dark terminal pane even when the user selects the light theme.

issues.description: The terminal surface background is still hard-coded to `Color(0xFF101418)`, so changing the app theme to Light does not update the live session surface consistently. This misses the feature requirement that theme preferences apply across persistent/live surfaces, including already-open sessions.

The terminal view keeps hard-coded preview text and background colors (`0xFFD7E3F4` / `0xFF101418`) instead of deriving them from the selected theme. As a result, the terminal pane remains visually dark under the Light theme, so the presentation is not applied consistently to the session surface.

summary: Reviewed the worker transcript skeleton, handoff, skill, and commit `0515dc8a26959acedfab10c5611e1437f171e175`. The implementation correctly persists theme/font preferences and updates session font metrics live, but it fails the feature as written because the terminal surface itself still uses hard-coded dark colors rather than the selected theme.
